questions.question_text: Seth has bought and ............ a loaf of bread.
questions.explanation: Has eaten

questions.question_text: My mum and............ visited Joseph.
questions.explanation:
Use I before a verb when you are the subject of the sentence as in the above sentence.... and I . example, Kofi and I like gardening, Adjei and I love action movies, My brother and I visited etc.

Use me after a verb, as in:

He gave me a lift home,

She gave Kofi and me a lift home,

She sends A

questions.question_text: The tiger............ in wait for its prey.
questions.explanation: ... laid in wait

questions.question_text: It's about time we......... studying hard.
questions.explanation:
it's about time is often followed by the past tense .

In this case the past tense of start which is started . So the correct answer is started .

 Example , It's about time that our country invested in education.

questions.question_text: Ben now wishes he............ his studies early.
questions.explanation:
The past tense of begin is began . Anytime that wish(es) is used, its followed by the past tense.

A wish about the present uses a simple past main verb. Example : He wishes he understood the lesson.

questions.question_text: This is exactly............ our mum tried to make you understand.
questions.explanation: The correct is all that and not all what. All what is colloquial (informal).

questions.question_text: Would you rather we ...... tomorrow?
She is my........... sister.
questions.explanation:
Don't say: Ama is my senior sister.

 Say: Ama is my elder sister.

 Don't say: Adjei is my junior brother.

 Say: Adjei is my younger brother.

questions.question_text: We cannot make tea because we have........... sugar left.

options.A:
little and little are used for uncountable nouns and a few and few for countable nouns . Sugar is uncountable and so either a little or very little is the possible answer.

Very little or very few means hardly any or not enough. Since it wasn't possible to make the tea, the appropriate answer is very little since there wasn't enough to make the tea.

questions.explanation:
A little and little are used for uncountable nouns and a few and few for countable nouns . Sugar is uncountable and so either a little or very little is the possible answer.

Very little or very few means hardly any or not enough. Since it wasn't possible to make the tea, the appropriate answer is v

questions.question_text: You should not tell.......... about our plans.
questions.explanation: We don’t use nobody, no one, nothing, nowhere after no, not, never or other words which have a negative meaning (hardly, seldom). We use anyone, anybody, anything, anywhere

questions.question_text: The leader........... suffer alone.
The flower pot is made.......... clay.
questions.explanation:
Made by identifies the person or process that made something. Made by John Boye , made by distillation .

 Made of tells us the substance that something is composed of. This table is made of wood .

questions.question_text: Let's go out and play,..........?
Sarah would have passed her examination........... she studied hard.
questions.explanation: the would have means she didn't pass her exams. She would had passed if she had studied hard

questions.question_text: The four brothers haven't seen........... since June last year.
questions.explanation:
We use the reciprocal pronouns each other and one another when two or more people do the same thing.

Peter and Mary helped each other .

= Peter helped Mary and Mary helped Peter.

We sent one another Christmas cards.

= We sent them a Christmas card and they sent us a Christmas card.

They did

 Antonyms (opposite in meaning) for fright 

Calm, confidence, contentment, happiness, bravery, calmness, courage, beauty, f

Analytical, curious, nosy, big-eyed, challenging, forward, impertinent, inquiring, inquisitorial, interested, intrusive, investigative, meddlesome, meddling, peering, personal, poking, presumptuous, probing, prying, questioning, scrutinizing, searching, si

questions.question_text:
I'm going to make you eat your words. 

 That means that you will.........

options.D: face the consequences of your actions

questions.explanation:
Eat your words be forced to admit that what you have said before was wrong

 Example : Nick told everyone that he'd be picked for the team, but when he wasn’t chosen he had to eat his words.

questions.question_text:
Those children were born with a silver spoon in their mouth. This means that the children were 
born.............

options.A: with their mouth full of silver.

questions.explanation:
born with a silver spoon 
Born into a wealthy family.

 Examples 

We may both be wealthy now, but I was not born with a silver spoon . I had nothing when I was young, and all of my fortune is down to my own hard work.

Everyone who attends that university was born with a silver spoon , so I just do

questions.question_text: The driver passed out soon after the accident occurred. This means that the driver..........
questions.explanation:
Pass out 

To fall asleep, faint, or lose consciousness.

 Examples 

He passed out as soon as he saw the blood.

They gave me so much to drink that I passed out cold by 10 PM.

I've been up since 5 AM, so I'm just going to go pass out after dinner.

questions.question_text: Susan was on edge after that incident. This means that she was.............
questions.explanation:
On edge 

Anxious, nervous and tense

 Example 

A: "Why is Carrie pacing?" B: "She's waiting for the doctor to call with her test results, so she's been on edge all day."

questions.question_text: We were taken to the police station like a lamb to the slaughter. This means that we were taken there.............
questions.explanation:
Like a lamb to the slaughter 

If someone goes somewhere like a lamb to the slaughter , they go there quietly and obediently because they have not realized that it will be dangerous or unpleasant, or because they are powerless.

 Example 

His young bride walked down the aisle like a lamb to the sla
